steps.title: Place Algeria in the argument
steps.content: The Algeria information gives an example of a major decline in solid-fuel use between 2000 and 2019. The text says such shifts are often explained by the energy ladder, a model that links fuel choice mainly to income. The Ghana study then complicates that model by showing fuel use was flexible and shaped by several factors.

steps.title: Match the critique of the model
steps.content: Choice A says the Algeria trend is poorly suited for evaluating the model, but the text uses it as the kind of trend the model tries to explain. Choice B invents two equally compelling explanations. Choice C claims Algeria and Ghana share a similar cause, which the passage does not say. The best description is that Algeria illustrates a phenomenon often explained by the energy ladder, while the later evidence suggests that explanation is inadequate. The answer is D.
